edges.description: Plaque disruption creates the local substrate for platelet deposition, coagulation cascade activation, and thrombus growth.
Persistent coronary flow obstruction deprives posterior left ventricular myocardium of oxygen and substrate, causing ischemic cardiomyocyte injury and infarction.
Posterior-wall injury is reflected by posterior-lead ST elevation and by reciprocal anterior precordial ST depression on standard 12-lead ECG.
Failure to recognize STEMI-negative occlusion MI patterns delays urgent catheterization and reperfusion despite similar PCI needs.

nodes.description: Rupture or erosion of an atherosclerotic coronary plaque can initiate acute coronary syndrome by exposing thrombogenic plaque contents and damaged endothelium to circulating blood.

evidence.reference_title: Thrombosis, physical activity, and acute coronary syndromes.
evidence.snippet: Acute coronary syndromes (ACS) are common, life-threatening cardiac disorders that typically are triggered by rupture or erosion of an atherosclerotic plaque.

nodes.description: After plaque disruption, platelet deposition and coagulation cascade activation form a platelet-fibrin thrombus. When this thrombus obstructs a posterior-wall supplying coronary artery, such as the circumflex or right coronary artery, it causes posterior myocardial ischemia or infarction.

evidence.reference_title: Thrombosis, physical activity, and acute coronary syndromes.
evidence.snippet: Platelet deposition and activation of the blood coagulation cascade in response to plaque disruption lead to the formation of a platelet-fibrin thrombus, which can grow rapidly, obstruct coronary blood flow, and cause myocardial ischemia and/or infarction.

evidence.reference_title: Occlusion of the infarct-related coronary artery presenting as acute coronary syndrome with and without ST-elevation: impact of inflammation and outcomes in a real-world prospective cohort
evidence.snippet: Hs-CRP, lymphocyte, and neutrophil counts were independent predictors of total IRA occlusion, suggesting a possible role of systemic inflammation in the detection of TCO irrespective of ECG presentation.

evidence.reference_title: Isolated posterior ST-elevation myocardial infarction: the necessity of routine 15-lead electrocardiography: a case series
evidence.snippet: Coronary angiography revealed a total/critical occlusion of the proximal circumflex coronary artery in all three cases.

nodes.description: Posterior-wall injury may be underdetected on standard 12-lead ECG because the posterior wall is not directly sampled. Reciprocal anterior ST-segment depression, especially maximal in V1-V4, and direct posterior-lead ST elevation identify posterior or posterolateral occlusion MI patterns that may not satisfy classic STEMI criteria.

evidence.reference_title: OMI/NOMI: Time for a New Classification of Acute Myocardial Infarction
evidence.snippet: Forty percent of patients with acute coronary occlusion myocardial infarction (OMI) do not present with STEMI criteria, which delays their treatment and increases morbidity and mortality.

nodes.description: Coronary occlusion reduces oxygen delivery to posterior left ventricular myocardium. Ischemia disrupts calcium handling and mitochondrial function in cardiomyocytes; reperfusion is essential for tissue salvage but can add ROS and ionic imbalance, with necrosis and apoptosis contributing to infarcted myocardium.

evidence.reference_title: Mitochondrial and mitochondrial-independent pathways of myocardial cell death during ischaemia and reperfusion injury
evidence.snippet: Acute myocardial infarction causes lethal injury to cardiomyocytes during both ischaemia and reperfusion (IR).

evidence.reference_title: Mitochondrial and mitochondrial-independent pathways of myocardial cell death during ischaemia and reperfusion injury
evidence.snippet: There is also evidence for significant myocardial death by other pathways such as apoptosis, although this has been challenged.

evidence.reference_title: Mitochondrial and mitochondrial-independent pathways of myocardial cell death during ischaemia and reperfusion injury
evidence.snippet: Necrosis is known to play a major role in myocardial IR injury.

evidence.reference_title: Mitochondrial and mitochondrial-independent pathways of myocardial cell death during ischaemia and reperfusion injury
evidence.snippet: Mitochondria play a central role in both of these pathways of cell death, as either a causal mechanism is the case of mitochondrial permeability transition leading to necrosis, or as part of the signalling pathway in mitochondrial cytochrome c release and apoptosis.

nodes.description: When posterior MI reflects acute coronary occlusion, timely primary PCI is the key reperfusion intervention. Posterior-lead ECG and OMI recognition are important because STEMI-negative occlusion patterns can otherwise experience delayed catheterization despite similar angiographic PCI needs.

evidence.reference_title: Isolated posterior ST-elevation myocardial infarction: the necessity of routine 15-lead electrocardiography: a case series
evidence.snippet: Routine and accurate interpretations of 15-lead electrocardiography (12-lead with additional V7–V9) resulted in a better sensitivity for isolated posterior myocardial infarction diagnoses, followed by a timely and opportune primary percutaneous coronary intervention.

evidence.reference_title: OMI/NOMI: Time for a New Classification of Acute Myocardial Infarction
evidence.snippet: STEMI(−)OMI patients had significant delays in catheterization, yet had angiographic findings, rates of PCI, and complications similar to STEMI(+)OMI.
